LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS VERONICA TEDESCHI AND GEORGE TEDESCHI'S ANTI-MOTION TO STRIKE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FROM PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. EVEN ASSUMING THAT DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN THAT THE COMPLAINT ARISES FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT A PRIMA FACIE CASE AS TO THE APPLICABILITY OF THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE BECAUSE THERE REMAINS A FACTUAL ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT INTENDED IN GOOD FAITH TO CARRY THROUGH WITH LITIGATION. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER, CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189

Mtn To Strike Fourth Cause Of Action From Pltf'S First Amended Complt; Req For Judicial Ntc
Off Calendar Jun-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ARTHUR C. LIPTON OF THE LAW OFFICES OF LIPTON, PIPER & SGANGA LLP, TEL. NO. 362-6286 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID SEMEL OF THE HAAPALA, THOMPSON & ABERN, LLP, TEL. NO. (510) 763-2324 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS VERONICA TEDESCHI AND GEORGE TEDESCHI'S ANTI-MOTION To Strike Fourth Cause Of Action From Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint IS DENIED. EVEN ASSUMING THAT DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN THAT THE COMPLAINT ARISES FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT A PRIMA FACIE CASE AS TO THE APPLICABILITY OF THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE BECAUSE THERE REMAINS A FACTUAL ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT INTENDED IN GOOD FAITH TO CARRY THROUGH WITH LITIGATION. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter, Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
